name: Navidrome MCP Server
what_it_does: Navidrome MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Navidrome — the self-hosted music streaming server — searching and querying personal music libraries, retrieving album and artist information, managing playlists, and integrating Navidrome's personal music collection into agent-driven music discovery and playlist management workflows.
use_cases: Searching personal music library for tracks from personal assistant agents
Creating and managing playlists from music curation agents
Querying album and artist metadata from music discovery agents
Generating music recommendations from personal library from recommendation agents
Building listening statistics from analytics agents
Managing personal music collection from productivity agents
not_for: Teams using Spotify, Apple Music, or other cloud streaming (use those MCPs)
Music streaming to speakers (playback control is client-dependent)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with a self-hosted Navidrome music server — for privacy-focused personal music library management and discovery without cloud streaming dependencies.
avoid_when: You use Spotify or other cloud streaming services — or don't run a self-hosted music server.

auth.notes: Navidrome/Subsonic API username + password authentication. MD5 salted auth for Subsonic API compatibility. Self-hosted Navidrome URL + credentials required.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free — Navidrome is open source (GPL)
pricing.notes: Navidrome is free and open source. MCP server is free. You only pay for hosting infrastructure.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted — limited by your server hardware; no external rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted Navidrome instance must be running and accessible
Subsonic API credentials use MD5 auth — ensure MCP handles salted auth correctly
Navidrome must have network access enabled for external agent connections
Library must be populated with music — fresh installations have empty library
Community MCP from individual contributor (Blakeem) — limited maintenance
Track/album IDs in Subsonic API are internal — not portable across Navidrome instances
agent_readiness.security_notes: Self-hosted Navidrome. Subsonic API with MD5 auth. Configure HTTPS for remote access. Community MCP. Store credentials as environment variables. Network-restrict Navidrome to trusted clients.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted. Configure HTTPS. MD5 auth. Store credentials securely. Community MCP.
